title: Whooping Cough Tests: MedlinePlus Medical Test
text:
throat. But in rare cases, your provider may use a procedure called a bronchoalveolar lavage (BAL) . This is a procedure that can be done during a bronchoscopy (a procedure that's used to check for the cause of a lung problem).
A bronchoalveolar lavage collects a sample from your lungs and checks it for the pertussis and parapertussis bacteria. Parapertussis is very similar to pertussis and can cause the same symptoms as those of whooping cough. The whooping cough vaccine does not protect you from parapertussis.
Your provider might order this test if:
You aren't improving with treatment for whooping cough. 
They suspect another respiratory illness, such as pneumonia. 
They need a tissue sample from the lower respiratory tract to see if your symptoms are being caused by a lower respiratory infection (LRTI).
